summary: Everyone has a loud opinion on the rent vs buy debate, especially online. But the truth is: there is no one-size-fits-all answer. In this episode cut, I bring together perspectives from Abhijit More, Lakshmi Iyer, and Aashiah Somaiyaa to decode what actually matters before you decide:

chapters.label: Rent vs Buy Is Not a Yes/No Question: Abhijit More
Lakshmi Iyer: One House Is Fine, Leverage Is the Real Risk
Aashish Somaiyaa: Life Stage, Balance Sheet & Timing Matter
Abhijit More: Ignore Black-and-White Influencer Advice

tool.label: Dream Home Plan
tool.reason: Run the honest version for your own home: how much you can afford, and what the EMI really costs your month.

disclaimer: This content is for educational purposes only and is not financial advice. Please consult a qualified financial advisor before making any investment decision.

seo.title: Should You Buy a House or Keep Renting? 3 Expert Views, No Noise
seo.description: Everyone has a loud opinion on the rent vs buy debate, especially online. But the truth is: there is no one-size-fits-all answer. In this episode cut, I

takeaways: There is no one-size-fits-all answer to rent vs buy; the right call depends on your life stage, cash flows and risk, not on loud opinions online.
The biggest mistake is over-leveraging: taking on a home loan while you are already carrying other loans.
Real estate is not like stocks or mutual funds; it is a high-involvement, family decision with both an emotional and a practical side.
If you want real estate exposure without buying a house, alternatives like REITs are worth a look.
The real question is whether you see a house as an investment or as security, and thinking it through beats generic influencer advice.

fork.decision: Should you buy a house or keep renting?

this.label: Buy the house
points: Owning your home carries real emotional and practical security that renting does not.
For most people one house makes sense when it fits your life stage and cash flows.
A home is a high-involvement family decision, not just a line on a spreadsheet.

that.label: Keep renting
points: Buying is a bad move if it forces you to over-leverage while you are already carrying loans.
Real estate is not flexible like stocks or mutual funds, so it can lock you in.
If you only want real estate exposure, REITs can offer it without buying property.

fork.pick: Buy one home for security when your life stage and cash flows can carry it without piling on debt; if you only want real estate as an investment, take the REIT route instead of stacking another property loan.
